a_image.alt: Recipe Card UI style mood board — Skeuomorphic · Culinary

sections.a_eyebrow: Hand-Crafted · Card Layout · Organic · Warm · Cozy
sections.a_description: A yellowed index card with handwritten headings, dotted lines for notes, and that warm, kitchen-counter feeling of a recipe passed down through generations — Recipe Card UI brings the intimate, hand-crafted quality of a family cookbook to the screen. The warm tones, handwriting-style typography, and simple card structure create an experience that feels personal, tested, and loved.

This aesthetic appears on food blogs, recipe sharing platforms, and cooking tutorial sites. You'll find it on farm-to-table restaurant menus, artisanal food brand pages, cooking class sign-up sites, and any culinary project that values the warmth and authenticity of grandma's kitchen over the sterility of a commercial cookbook.

a_image_cover.alt: Recipe Card UI style use case illustration
Recipe Card UI style use case illustration
Recipe Card UI style use case illustration

sections.a_description: A vintage recipe card — the kind your grandmother kept in a wooden box, slightly yellowed with age, handwritten notes in the margins, and a warm kitchen aesthetic. Background: soft warm white (#faf7f0). The .inner panel simulates a 4x6 index card: off-white (#fffef8) background with a thin border (#e0d5c0), gentle rounded corners (6px), and a subtle drop shadow as if the card is sitting on a kitchen counter. A top accent stripe of warm red-orange (#d35400) 4px thick — like the colored edge of a recipe card divider. Dotted horizontal lines across the body area for that ruled-card feel. Typography is warm and readable. Set in Merriweather for body — a serif designed for screen readability with a warm, friendly character. Caveat for the heading — handwriting font giving it that grandma's-recipe-book authenticity. Heading in Caveat at 30px, line-height 1.2, color #3e2723 (chocolate brown). Eyebrow in Merriweather at 11px, weight 700, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #d35400 (warm red-orange). Body at 15px, line-height 1.7, color #4a3728. Buttons: primary — warm red-orange (#d35400), cream text, 6px border-radius. Secondary: transparent, warm brown border. Alternatives: Libre Baskerville, Source Serif 4, Lora.
